title: Free the States Has Closed It's Doors... What's Next? Abolitionists Rising!
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman announces that Free the States is closing and the team is restructuring under a new organization, Abolitionists Rising, to refocus on building a nationwide movement through cultural engagement, education, and national repentance. The post outlines the new organization's plans for a website, expanded media, a 2024 conference, and continued activism resources.
audiences: Existing Free the States donors and email subscribers
Abolitionist activists looking for organizational direction
Pro-life Christians considering moving toward abolitionism
Curious observers of the abolitionist movement's strategy
Pastors and church leaders evaluating the movement

questions.question: Why is Free the States closing its doors?
questions.audience: Existing Free the States donors and email subscribers
questions.answer: The current cultural and political landscape requires a refocused strategy emphasizing nationwide movement-building, cultural engagement, education, and national repentance, which is best pursued under a new organization rather than continuing FTS's prior structure focused primarily on state sovereignty and legislative initiatives.
questions.quote: To best achieve these long-held goals, FTS is closing it's doors and we are restructuring under a new organization, Abolitionists Rising.

questions.question: What was Free the States' original strategic focus before the transition?
questions.audience: Curious observers of the abolitionist movement's strategy
questions.answer: Since 2018, Free the States pursued abolition primarily by asserting state sovereignty and pushing legislative initiatives, work the team credits with significant progress for preborn children.
questions.quote: Since 2018, Free the States sought to abolish abortion through various projects primarily focused on asserting state sovereignty and legislative initiatives.

questions.question: What kind of movement is Abolitionists Rising trying to build?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activists looking for organizational direction
questions.answer: A robust, nationwide movement united under biblical ideology and uncompromising commitment to Christ, with Abolitionists Rising acting as both catalyst and resource hub for abolitionists across the country.
questions.quote: We strive to help build a movement united under biblical ideology and commitment to Christ, never compromising on those core principles.

questions.question: What is the legal and organizational status of Abolitionists Rising?
questions.audience: Existing Free the States donors and email subscribers
questions.answer: Abolitionists Rising is currently active and pursuing 501(c)3 tax-deductible status, will retain former Free the States staff, and has assembled a board of directors.
questions.quote: AR is currently active and is in the process of becoming a 501(c)3 tax-deductible organization. It will maintain former FTS staff and has established a robust board of directors.

questions.question: What new resources and content can supporters expect from Abolitionists Rising?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering moving toward abolitionism
questions.answer: A new website with a knowledge center, The Norman Statement, news updates, and local connection tools; expanded social media (already nearing two million daily views); more educational videos, podcasts, and documentaries; and an overhauled gear store.
questions.quote: We are building a website that will contain many resources, including a knowledge center, The Norman Statement, regular news updates, and ways to connect with other abolitionists in your area.

questions.question: When and where is the next major in-person abolitionist gathering?
questions.audience: Abolitionist activists looking for organizational direction
questions.answer: The 2024 Abolition Now Conference will be held in Charlotte, North Carolina from April 18-21, and the team intends to make it the biggest and most culturally engaging conference yet.
questions.quote: Also, mark your calendars for the 2024 Abolition Now Conference in Charlotte, North Carolina April 18-21, which we intend to make the biggest and most culturally engaging conference yet!

questions.question: What should existing Free the States donors do about their giving?
questions.audience: Existing Free the States donors and email subscribers
questions.answer: Donations to Free the States have been ceased; supporters are asked to redirect their giving to Abolitionists Rising via AbolitionistsRising.com/Donate, ideally as a monthly contribution.
questions.quote: Donations to Free the States have all been ceased, but your support to Abolitionists Rising is critical to keep us driving forward.

questions.question: Why does the post emphasize signing up for email updates?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians considering moving toward abolitionism
questions.answer: Big tech platforms have decreased the team's social media reach in recent months, so email is the most reliable channel for receiving important content and abolition bill news.
questions.quote: Big tech is cracking down on our ability to reach you! Our social media reach has been greatly decreased in recent months.
